page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr6.10xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 40 dedicated physical cores utilizing the Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 9.6 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates solid processing capabilities, achieving a stress-ng single-core score of 1436.39 and a multi-core score of 38318.39. Memory performance is highlighted by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 2128.36 GB/sec and a large block uncached write bandwidth of 49.57 GB/sec. Database operations are supported by a PassMark database score of 14328.81 and Redis SET performance reaching 32.74 million operations per second. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the system processes 135M model prompts at 1934.13 tokens per second and 7B model prompts at 56.33 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.hfr6.10xlarge offers a high density of dedicated memory resources relative to its CPU allocation, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of local storage maintenance. However, the lack of local NVMe SSDs means all storage operations must rely on network-attached volumes, which represents a key architectural tradeoff. This instance is best suited for memory-intensive applications, including in-memory databases like Redis, enterprise applications, large-scale caching layers, and data processing workloads that do not require local disk storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr6.10xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 40 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 3.1 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via KVM, this instance provides 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 9.6 GB. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong memory performance, with cached read bandwidth reaching 2128.36 GB/sec and Redis SET operations executing at 32.74 million operations per second. The instance delivers solid multi-core processing, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 38318.39. This configuration is highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, such as in-memory databases, large-scale caching, enterprise application hosting, and data processing tasks that demand substantial memory capacity without requiring local physical disks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr6.10xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 40 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 384 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for in-memory databases and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high memory density for demanding database and caching workloads.

384.0 GB of RAM providing 9.6 GB per core memory density
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications
